alert_dynamic-table-must-have-sorted-and: Dynamic table must have at least one sorted column and one unsorted column
alert_dynamic-tables-no-descending-colum: Dynamic tables do not support descending-sorted columns
alert_dynamic-tables-no-descending-order: Dynamic tables do not support 'Descending' order
alert_key-column-wrong-type: Key-column should not be of type [{{dataType}}]
alert_no-suggestions-yet: There is no any suggestions yet
alert_queue-no-sorted-columns: Queue must not have any sorted columns
alert_replicas-count-must-be-integer: The value must be defined as a valid integer number
alert_replicas-count-out-of-range: The value must be in range [0; 10]
alert_column-name-already-exists: The column with this name already exists
alert_column-must-be-optional: Column with type [{{dataType}}] should be optional.
alert_name-matches-parent-directory: The name must not match to parent directory name
alert_unique-keys-needs-sorted-column: The table must contain at least one sorted column.
alert_lock-cannot-be-set-on-key-column: Lock cannot be set on a key-column
alert_aggregate-invalid-type: [{{aggr}}] aggregate might be only applied to a column of type int64/uint64/double
alert_aggregate-xdelta-invalid-type: [{{aggr}}] aggregate might be only applied to a column of type string
context_before-using-dynamic-tables: Before using Dynamic tables in production environment with
context_consult-with-dev-team: you have to consult with development team.
context_dynamic-tables-allow-modify: which allow to modify data rows by key.
context_erasure-encoding-description: Erasure encoding allows to minimize disk space allocated for the table comparing with Replication, but it requires more CPU time during data access.
context_lock-description: By default locking is applied to all columns of row. This parameter allows to improve granularity of locking by defining groups of columns to lock.
context_optional-description: If the parameter is enabled the value might be defined as null. For dynamic tables all key-columns are required regardless of this value.
context_same-group-same-block: When columns have the same group name then the data of such columns will be placed in the same blocks.
context_static-tables-description: nearly impossible to modify, but they might be removed or merged, also new data rows might be appended to the end of a table. As the opposite of the above described restrictions there are
context_unique-keys-description: The parameter is actual only for static tables, for all dynamic tables it is always automatically enabled regardless of its value.
